steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: The student wants to "emphasize the order in which the two lighthouse keepers began their careers." The correct choice must explicitly show which keeper started first and which started later — not when careers ended, not general praise, not navigation impact.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the one that shows career-start order
steps.content:
Check each choice against the goal:
  • Choice A — 'From 1867 to 1918, the nighttime waters of Mississippi were more navigable thanks to lighthouse keepers Flora McNeil and Maria Younghans.': focuses on navigation impact and misattributes McNeil to Mississippi — does not establish start order. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Before automation, lighthouse keepers like Maria Younghans and Flora McNeil were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.': makes a general statement about both keepers; does not compare when either began. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Flora McNeil began her career as a lighthouse keeper years after Maria Younghans did.': directly states that Younghans started first (1867) and McNeil started later (1904), establishing the chronological order of career starts. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Maria Younghans's career as a lighthouse keeper ended in 1918, whereas Flora McNeil's ended in 1920.': compares when careers ended, not when they began. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
